intro: Arthrosamanea altissima, also known as Saman tree or Rain tree, is a large deciduous tree native to tropical Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Arthrosamanea altissima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets in modern herbal formulations.
